steps.title: Recognize the evidence threshold
steps.content: The passage identifies 400°C as the critical temperature—the typical campfire temperature that the team used to assess whether artifacts show evidence of fire control.

steps.title: Locate the qualifying artifacts
steps.content: The graph shows L5_239 and K3_18 at roughly 550°C and 650°C respectively, both exceeding 400°C and supporting the team's fire-control conclusion. The other choices involve below-threshold artifacts, while C sets an irrelevant standard. This confirms B is correct.
